ground_truth.title: RESTAURANT | definition in the Cambridge English Dictionary
ground_truth.main_content: RESTAURANT meaning: 1. a place where meals are prepared and served to customers. The word 'restaurant' is a noun, countable. American English pronunciation: /ˈres.tə.rɑːnt/, British English pronunciation: /ˈres.tər.ɒnt/. A restaurant is a place of business where people can choose a meal to be prepared and served to them at a table, and for which they pay, usually after eating. Examples: We went to a little Italian restaurant and shared a pizza. Does the hotel have a restaurant? Smoking is not allowed in this restaurant. They do really good food at that restaurant and it's not very expensive either. COMPARISON: eatery (informal). Related vocabulary: all-you-can-eat, automat, bar, bistro, diner, drive-through, restaurateur, wine list. BUSINESS: the restaurant business/industry, a restaurant chain, a restaurant owner/manager. Collocations: barbecue restaurant, drive-in restaurant, ethnic restaurant.
with: a place where meals are prepared and served to customers
restaurant is a noun referring to a place of business where people can choose a meal to be prepared and served to them at a table
We went to a little Italian restaurant and shared a pizza
Smoking is not allowed in this restaurant
Compare eatery (informal) for the meaning of restaurant
